delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

summary: This is an introductory level course. ICC prepares language learners to develop a critical awareness of how their own communicative patterns influence communication with people from other cultures. ICC is an interdisciplinary field, drawing from anthropology, linguistics, psychology, and communication studies all of which are important for language learners. Students will be introduced to these influences during this course. In this course, there will be a constant negotiation of focus between the specialized course content and the English language needs of the students. ICC content themes include pragmatics, nonverbal communication, ICC competence, cross-cultural interaction, and analysis of communicative culture.
aims:
1. Analyze and compare Japanese cultural influences with North American communicative culture at introductory level.
2. Develop critical awareness of communicative patterns between cultures at an introductory level.
3. Have a basic understand the work of leading ICC scholars

Develop a critical awareness of how their own communicative patterns influence communication with people from other cultures.

Develop critical awareness of communicative patterns between cultures at an introductory level.

plan.text:
Course introduction:
Course intro, expectations, assessment philosophy, explanation of student cards

An introduction to Intercultural Communication: Definitions, Themes, Processes
Why the Study of Intercultural Communication is Important for Language Learning
Culture's influence on language and the work of Edward T. Hall: Origins of the ICC field
American/Japanese perceptions of each other's communicative behavior: John Condon's findings
Social interaction with non-Japanese: Readings from Mike Guest
Differences in interactional format: persuasion vs. harmonization
Self-awareness tables for IC Communication I
The process of intercultural adaptation: Culture shock (I)
The process of intercultural adaptation: Culture shock (II)
Course reflection and consolidation, Q&A session.

textbooks:
The International Encyclopedia of Intercultural Communication, 3 Volume Set
Young Yun Kim (Editor)
ISBN: 978-1-118-78394-8
prereq: Open to second year students and above. Students will be expected to complete all assignments on time using English only. Students will be expected to do all activities, tasks, assignments, readings, and reports in English. Any student taking this course should have motivation and interest in English and Intercultural Communication, and English listening skills sufficient for understanding an English-medium lecture.
prep: Students will expected to prepare for class by doing assigned academic readings and preparatory tasks throughout the course. Several homework assignments will be set as part of the course content. Independent work outside of class time (readings, tasks, homework/preparation) is a fundamental requirement for this course.
teachers: ◎ ダレン リングリー
